language_detection_guide.overview: Comprehensive rules and strategies for automatically detecting the primary programming language of a project to determine appropriate QSE scan configuration.

language.name: Java
language.special_requirements: Requires class file path (-cf) for Cryptographic Analytics

step.title: Use list_files Tool
step.description: Get a recursive listing of all files in the project directory

step.title: Count File Extensions
step.description: Analyze the file listing to count occurrences of each supported extension

step.title: Identify Primary Language
step.description: Determine which language has the most source files

rules.rule: Language with highest count of source files is primary
If counts are close (within 20%), check for language-specific markers
If still ambiguous, ask user to specify language

step.title: Verify with Language Markers
step.description: Confirm detection by checking for language-specific project files

algorithm.name: simple_count
algorithm.description: Basic detection based on file extension counts
algorithm.pseudocode: 1. Get recursive file listing 2. Initialize counters for each extension 3. For each file: - If file is in excluded directory, skip - If file has .java extension, increment java_count - If file has .go extension, increment go_count - If file has .dart extension, increment dart_count - If file has .py extension, increment python_count - If file has .cpp/.cc/.cxx extension, increment cpp_count 4. Return language with highest count 5. If no files found, return null (ask user)

algorithm.name: weighted_detection
algorithm.description: Advanced detection considering file locations and markers
algorithm.pseudocode: 1. Perform simple_count algorithm 2. Check for language-specific markers: - If pom.xml or build.gradle exists, boost Java score - If go.mod exists, boost Go score - If pubspec.yaml exists, boost Dart score - If requirements.txt or setup.py exists, boost Python score - If CMakeLists.txt exists, boost C++ score 3. Check source file locations: - Files in src/main/java/ strongly indicate Java - Files in cmd/ or pkg/ indicate Go - Files in lib/ with .dart indicate Dart - Files in src/ with .py indicate Python 4. Return language with highest weighted score 5. If ambiguous (scores within 20%), ask user

case.name: polyglot_project

strategy.step: Identify the dominant language by file count
Check if user wants to scan specific language
If unclear, ask user which language to scan

case.example: Project has both Java backend and Python scripts. If Java files outnumber Python 3:1, default to Java. Otherwise, ask user which to scan.

strategy.step: Report that no supported language was detected
List supported languages: Java, Go, Dart, Python, C++
Ask user to specify language or provide correct path

case.name: test_files_only
case.description: Only test files found, no main source files

strategy.step: Count test files to determine language
Warn user that only test files were found
Use .cpp as the standard extension for QSE
Note: QSE will scan all C++ variants

pattern.typical_structure: src/main/java/ - Main source files src/test/java/ - Test files target/ - Build output (Maven) build/ - Build output (Gradle)
cmd/ - Command line applications pkg/ - Library packages internal/ - Private packages vendor/ - Dependencies
lib/ - Library code bin/ - Executables test/ - Tests web/ - Web files (Flutter)
src/ or package_name/ - Source code tests/ - Test files venv/ or .venv/ - Virtual environment
src/ - Source files include/ - Header files build/ - Build output lib/ - Libraries

fallback_strategy.when: Language detection is uncertain or fails
fallback_strategy.action: Use ask_followup_question to request language specification from user
fallback_strategy.question_template: I couldn't automatically detect the programming language of this project. Which language should I scan for quantum safety?

suggestions.suggest: Java (.java) - Supports both Cryptographic Analytics and API Discovery

validation_rules.rule: Always verify detected language makes sense for the project structure
If file count is very low (< 5 files), consider asking user for confirmation
If multiple languages have similar counts (within 20%), check for markers
Never assume language without evidence from file system
When in doubt, ask the user rather than guessing
